name: Log Cabin Classic
description: The timeless log cabin block — strips around a center square. A perfect first quilt project that teaches accurate seam allowances and pressing.
summary: Build 20 log cabin blocks from a center square outward, adding light and dark strips in rounds. Arrange blocks in a Barn Raising setting for a dramatic on-point effect, then add borders and finish.

pieces.notes: Sub-cut into varying lengths as you build each round
Sub-cut into varying lengths as you build each round

steps.title: Prepare Center Squares
steps.instruction: Cut 20 center squares at 2 1/2" x 2 1/2" from the red solid fabric. Press all squares with starch for stability.

tips.text: A little spray starch now prevents stretching later.

steps.title: Cut Light and Dark Strips
steps.instruction: Cut all light and dark print fabrics into 2 1/2" x WOF (Width of Fabric) strips. You'll sub-cut these to length as you build each block.

warnings.message: Keep light and dark strips separated — mixing them up changes the block design.

tips.text: Stack up to 4 layers and cut multiple strips at once to save time.

steps.title: First Round — Light Strips
steps.instruction: Place a center square RST (Right Sides Together) with a light strip. Sew with a 1/4" seam along one side. Press seam toward the strip. Trim strip even with the square. Rotate 90° and add a second light strip the same way.

tips.text: Use a 1/4" presser foot for consistent seams throughout.

steps.title: First Round — Dark Strips
steps.instruction: Rotate 90° again. Add a dark strip RST along the next side. Sew, press toward dark, trim. Rotate 90° and add the fourth strip (dark) to complete Round 1. Your unit should now measure 4 1/2" square.

warnings.message: Check your block measures 4 1/2" square after Round 1. If it's off, check your seam allowance.

tips.text: Always press toward the most recently added strip.

steps.title: Continue Building Rounds
steps.instruction: Repeat the pattern: 2 light strips, then 2 dark strips per round, always rotating 90° clockwise after each strip. Complete 3 full rounds total. Each finished block should measure 12 1/2" x 12 1/2" unfinished.

warnings.message: Square up each block to 12 1/2" after the final round. Blocks must be identical for the quilt to lay flat.

tips.text: Chain-piece all 20 blocks through each step to save time and thread.
Use a square ruler to check squareness after each round.

steps.title: Arrange Blocks — Barn Raising Layout
steps.instruction: Arrange all 20 blocks in 5 rows of 4, rotating blocks so the light sides form concentric diamonds radiating from the center (Barn Raising setting). Take a photo of your layout before sewing.

tips.text: Use a design wall or lay blocks on a bed. Step back and squint to check the overall pattern.

steps.title: Sew Rows Together
steps.instruction: Sew blocks into rows, pressing seams in alternating directions (odd rows left, even rows right). Then join rows, nesting seams at intersections. Press row seams open or to one side.

warnings.message: Pin at every seam intersection before sewing rows together.

tips.text: Alternating press directions lets seams nest together for perfect intersections.

steps.title: Add Borders
steps.instruction: Measure your quilt top through the center (not the edges). Sew side borders first, then top and bottom. Press seams toward the border fabric.

warnings.message: Always measure through the center — edges can stretch and give a false measurement.

tips.text: Pin borders at the center, then quarters, then eighths for even distribution.

steps.title: Baste and Quilt
steps.instruction: Make a quilt sandwich: backing (wrong side up), batting, quilt top (right side up). Baste with pins or spray. Quilt as desired — stitch in the ditch along block seams works beautifully for log cabins.

warnings.message: Backing and batting should extend 2-3 inches beyond the quilt top on all sides.

tips.text: For a beginner-friendly option, stitch in the ditch along the block seams and borders.

steps.title: Bind the Quilt
steps.instruction: Join binding strips end to end with diagonal seams. Press in half lengthwise, wrong sides together. Attach to quilt front with 1/4" seam, mitering corners. Fold to back and hand-stitch or machine-stitch in place.

tips.text: Diagonal seams on binding joins distribute bulk better than straight seams.
Add a label to the back with your name, date, and the recipient!
